message:
chore(deps): migrate asupersync + franken-* to crates.io v0.3.0 and drop local-path [patch] blocks

Up until now, mcp_agent_mail_rust declared `asupersync = "0.2.9"` at
[workspace.dependencies] but forced everything through two
`[patch]` blocks pointing at local `../asupersync` paths — so the
package couldn't build without a sibling checkout and the lockfile
carried the patched path entries. With asupersync v0.3.0 now
published to crates.io (workspace-wide: asupersync +
asupersync-macros + franken-kernel/evidence/decision), the patch
indirection is no longer needed.

- Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]:
  `asupersync = { version = "0.2.9", features = ["tls-webpki-roots"] }`
  → `{ version = "0.3.0", features = ["tls-webpki-roots"] }`.
- Cargo.toml [patch.crates-io]: remove the asupersync /
  asupersync-macros / franken-kernel / franken-evidence /
  franken-decision entries that redirected to `../asupersync`
  sibling paths. The `beads_rust = { path = "../beads_rust" }` patch
  and the sqlmodel/fsqlite/ftui sibling patches stay — those are
  separate workstreams with their own release cadences.
- Cargo.toml: remove the `[patch."https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync"]`
  block (asupersync-macros path override). Once downstream crates
  that git-pin asupersync (e.g. old frankensearch revs) re-release
  against v0.3.0, the dep graph will unify through crates.io alone.
- Cargo.lock: refreshed via `cargo update`, resolving asupersync and
  franken-* to the crates.io 0.3.0 artifacts (no more path sources).

Unrelated edit under tests/artifacts/perf/ from a concurrent
profiling run is NOT included.
